note: The upgrade-readiness check definitions (DQL + JS) the skill runs directly against the tenant via dtctl. This is the single source of truth — the skill never fetches any dashboard. To refresh when Dynatrace updates the checks, an internal maintainer can re-export the tile set and replace `content`.

0.content:
### See which classic features need migrating

---

Use this dashboard to assess classic feature usage and track your environment's readiness to migrate to the latest Dynatrace.
[Upgrade guide](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/upgrade-latest-dynatrace#what-you-need-to-know-first)

**Status colours** mean the same everywhere: 🟢 ready · 🟡 in progress · 🔴 action required before upgrading · ⚪ not applicable / no data. The label next to each colour names the specific state — open a tile's ⓘ for what it means and how to fix it.

5.content:
### Management zone usage

---

Management zones are classic functionality and will be removed.

**Action**: Review usage to plan your migration to [segments](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/manage/segments/upgrade-guide-segments) and IAM policies.

12.content:
### Classic App usage

---

Most of the classic apps will be removed in the latest Dynatrace. Identify active users of deprecated apps and guide them to replacement apps.

**Action**: [Upgrade from Dashboards Classic](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/upgrade-guide-dashboards) and [restrict users to the latest Dynatrace apps](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/upgrade-guide-prevent-classic)

14.content:
### OpenPipeline adoption

---

Classic processing pipelines will be removed in the latest Dynatrace. The chart tracks how much of your log and business event volume still flows through the classic pipeline versus OpenPipeline, and the table lists each data type with its migration status.

**Action**: [Migrate classic processing rules to OpenPipeline](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/migrate-classic-pipelines).

20.title: Classic vs OpenPipeline record volume

21.description:
Ingest still landing on classic pipelines vs. moved to OpenPipeline, per data type.

🟢 **Ready** — no classic records; fully on OpenPipeline. \
🟡 **In progress** — partially migrated; finish moving ingest to OpenPipeline. \
🔴 **Action required** — still on the classic pipeline; migrate this data type before upgrading.
21.query:
// Migration status of classic processing pipelines for logs and business events. Each row is a data
// type still eligible for OpenPipeline migration. "Classic records" counts records still processed by
// the legacy classic pipeline (the default route) in the selected timeframe, and "% migrated" is the
// share already handled by OpenPipeline routes. A data type is ready once no records use the classic
// pipeline.
timeseries total = sum(dt.sfm.openpipeline.routing.records, default: 0),
  by: { configuration },
  filter: { in(configuration, {"logs", "bizevents"}) },
  bins: 24
// Left-outer join keeps a row for fully-migrated data types (no classic records) instead of dropping them.
| join [
    timeseries classic = sum(dt.sfm.openpipeline.routing.records, default: 0),
      by: { configuration },
      filter: { in(configuration, {"logs", "bizevents"}) and matchesValue(route_name, "default") },
      bins: 24
  ], on: { configuration }, fields: { classic }, kind:leftOuter
| fieldsAdd `Classic records` = coalesce(arraySum(classic), 0)
| fieldsAdd total_records = arraySum(total)
| fieldsAdd `% migrated` = if(total_records > 0, (total_records - `Classic records`) * 100.0 / total_records, else: 100.0)
| fieldsAdd `Usage trend` = coalesce(classic, iCollectArray(total[] * 0))
| fieldsAdd Status = if(`Classic records` == 0, "🟢 Ready", else: if(`% migrated` <= 0, "🔴 Action required", else: "🟡 In progress"))
| fieldsAdd `Data type` = if(configuration == "logs", "Logs", else: if(configuration == "bizevents", "Business events", else: configuration))
| fieldsAdd `Data type` = concat(
  "[", `Data type`, "]",
  "(/ui/apps/dynatrace.settings/settings/openpipeline-", configuration, "/pipelines)")
| fields `Data type`, `Usage trend`, timeframe, interval, `Classic records`, `% migrated`, Status
| sort `Classic records` desc
21.title: OpenPipeline migration by data type

39.description:
Classic apps without a ready replacement on the latest Dynatrace, ranked by how heavily they're used so you can prioritise migration.

🔴 **High usage** — heavily used; plan its migration first. \
🟡 **Moderate usage** — migrate after the high-usage apps. \
⚪ **Low usage** — little used; lowest priority.

40.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.provider == "CLASSIC_APPS"
| filter event.type == "app.opened"
| filter isNotNull(dt.app.id)
| lookup [
  data record( AppStatus = decodeBase64ToString($AppsStatus:triplequote) )
  | parse AppStatus, """JSON_ARRAY:AppStatus"""
  | expand AppStatus
  | fieldsAdd describtion = AppStatus[`description`]
], sourceField:dt.app.id, lookupField:AppStatus[appId]
| fieldsAdd available = isNull(lookup.AppStatus)

| fieldsAdd substring(dt.app.id, from: 0), alias: `app ID`
| fieldsAdd `Name` = coalesce(lookup.describtion, dt.app.id)

| makeTimeseries Usage = count(default:0),
    bins: 10,
    by:{`Name`, dt.app.id, available}

| fieldsAdd totalUsage = arraySum(Usage)
| filterOut available
| sort totalUsage desc

48.title: Kubernetes monitoring prerequisites
48.description:
Each Kubernetes monitoring feature must stay on for full observability after you upgrade. Rows are scopes — the environment default, then any cluster override that still needs action.

49.content:
### Application Security

Enable the new monitoring rules to start using the new [Vulnerabilities app](/ui/apps/dynatrace.security.vulnerabilities), which helps you understand each detected vulnerability and prioritize what to fix first. Enabling the new rules disables the classic rules; to revert, disable the new monitoring rules.

**Action**: [Upgrade Vulnerability Analytics from classic to latest](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/secure/application-security/vulnerability-analytics/vulnerabilities-upgrade-classic-to-latest)

52.title: Enable new monitoring rules
52.description:
Whether the new Application Security monitoring rules are enabled for this environment.

🟢 **Enabled** — new monitoring rules are on; ready. \
🔴 **Disabled** — turn on the new monitoring rules before upgrading.

53.title: Required environment settings
53.description:
Runtime vulnerability detection settings that should be on for the new Application Security, read at environment scope.

🔴 **Disabled** — turn this on before upgrading to keep the matching vulnerability coverage. \
🟢 **Enabled** — ready. \
⚪ **Not found** — no value was returned for this setting.

70.query:
// REST/settings table: deprecated classic REST API + settings calls, grouped by caller.
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "CLASSIC_API"
    or (event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
        and startsWith(resource, "/platform/classic/")
        and (in(dt.app.id, {"dynatrace.dashboards", "dynatrace.notebooks", "dynatrace.davis.anomalydetection", "dynatrace.automations"})
            or isNotNull(details.dt.automation_engine.workflow.id)))
| fieldsAdd resource = if(event.provider == "API_GATEWAY", replaceString(resource, "/platform/classic/environment-api", "/api"), else: resource)
| fieldsAdd resource = if(event.provider == "API_GATEWAY", replaceString(resource, "/platform/classic/config-api", "/api/config"), else: resource)
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "UUIDSTRING", "{uuid}")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "SMARTSCAPEID", "{entity}")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'CREDENTIALS_VAULT-'[A-Z0-9]+", "CREDENTIALS_VAULT-{id}")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/'[a-zA-Z]{3} [0-9]{5} '/'", "/{tenant}/")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/vu'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{20,}", "/vu{settingsId}")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "('/dynatrace.' | '/my.')[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+", "/{appId}")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "(INT '.')(INT '.')(INT)('-' LD)? '/'", "/{appVersion}/")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/com.dynatrace.'LD:ext ('/' | EOS)", "/{extension}/")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/com.dynatrace.custom.'LD:ext ('/' | EOS)", "/{extension}/")
| fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "INT '_' INT 'V' INT", "{problemId}")
| fieldsAdd category = if(event.provider == "CLASSIC_API", "API tokens",
    else: if(isNotNull(details.dt.automation_engine.workflow.id), "Workflows",
    else: if(dt.app.id == "dynatrace.dashboards", "Dashboards",
    else: if(dt.app.id == "dynatrace.notebooks", "Notebooks",
    else: if(dt.app.id == "dynatrace.davis.anomalydetection", "Anomaly Detectors",
    else: "Other")))))
| fieldsAdd document_id = if(event.provider == "CLASSIC_API", authentication.token,
    else: if(isNotNull(details.dt.automation_engine.workflow.id), details.dt.automation_engine.workflow.id,
    else: concat("(all ", category, ")")))
| fieldsAdd fakeField = 1
| join [
    data record(AppStatus = decodeBase64ToString($ApiStatus:triplequote))
    | parse AppStatus, """JSON_ARRAY:AppStatus"""
    | expand AppStatus
    | fields priority = AppStatus[priority], apiPath = AppStatus[apiPath], behavior = AppStatus[behavior]
    | fieldsAdd fakeField = 1
  ], on: {fakeField}, kind:leftOuter
| fieldsAdd behavior = right.behavior, apiPath = right.apiPath, priority = right.priority
| filter startsWith(resource, apiPath)
| sort priority asc
| summarize behavior = takeFirst(behavior), by: {timestamp, resource, category, document_id}
| filter behavior == "BLOCK"
| fields timestamp, category, document_id
| makeTimeseries executions = count(), default: 0, by: {document_id, category}, bins: 24
| fieldsAdd total = arraySum(executions)
| lookup [
    fetch dt.system.events
    | filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
    | filter event.provider == "CLASSIC_API"
        or (event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
            and startsWith(resource, "/platform/classic/")
            and (in(dt.app.id, {"dynatrace.dashboards", "dynatrace.notebooks", "dynatrace.davis.anomalydetection", "dynatrace.automations"})
                or isNotNull(details.dt.automation_engine.workflow.id)))
    | fieldsAdd resource = if(event.provider == "API_GATEWAY", replaceString(resource, "/platform/classic/environment-api", "/api"), else: resource)
    | fieldsAdd resource = if(event.provider == "API_GATEWAY", replaceString(resource, "/platform/classic/config-api", "/api/config"), else: resource)
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "UUIDSTRING", "{uuid}")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "SMARTSCAPEID", "{entity}")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'CREDENTIALS_VAULT-'[A-Z0-9]+", "CREDENTIALS_VAULT-{id}")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/'[a-zA-Z]{3} [0-9]{5} '/'", "/{tenant}/")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/vu'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{20,}", "/vu{settingsId}")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "('/dynatrace.' | '/my.')[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+", "/{appId}")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "(INT '.')(INT '.')(INT)('-' LD)? '/'", "/{appVersion}/")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/com.dynatrace.'LD:ext ('/' | EOS)", "/{extension}/")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "'/com.dynatrace.custom.'LD:ext ('/' | EOS)", "/{extension}/")
    | fieldsAdd resource = replacePattern(resource, "INT '_' INT 'V' INT", "{problemId}")
    | fieldsAdd document_id = if(event.provider == "CLASSIC_API", authentication.token,
        else: if(isNotNull(details.dt.automation_engine.workflow.id), details.dt.automation_engine.workflow.id,
        else: concat("(all ", if(dt.app.id == "dynatrace.dashboards", "Dashboards",
          else: if(dt.app.id == "dynatrace.automations", "Workflows",
          else: if(dt.app.id == "dynatrace.notebooks", "Notebooks",
          else: "Anomaly Detectors"))), ")")))
    | summarize event_count = count(), by: {resource, document_id}
    | fieldsAdd fakeField = 1
    | join [
        data record(AppStatus = decodeBase64ToString($ApiStatus:triplequote))
        | parse AppStatus, """JSON_ARRAY:AppStatus"""
        | expand AppStatus
        | fields priority = AppStatus[priority], apiPath = AppStatus[apiPath], behavior = AppStatus[behavior]
        | fieldsAdd fakeField = 1
      ], on: {fakeField}, kind:leftOuter
    | fieldsAdd behavior = right.behavior, apiPath = right.apiPath, priority = right.priority
    | filter startsWith(resource, apiPath)
    | sort priority asc
    | summarize behavior = takeFirst(behavior), apiPath = takeFirst(apiPath), event_count = takeFirst(event_count), by: {resource, document_id}
    | filter behavior == "BLOCK"
    | summarize violation_count = sum(event_count), by: {document_id, violation_label = apiPath}
    | fieldsAdd label = concat(violation_label, " (", toString(toLong(violation_count)), ")")
    | summarize labels = collectDistinct(label), by: {document_id}
  ], sourceField: document_id, lookupField: document_id
| fieldsAdd `Deprecated usages` = lookup.labels

| sort total desc
| limit 500
| fieldsAdd Source = if(category == "Dashboards",
    if(startsWith(document_id, "(all"), "(all dashboards — REST API)", else: concat("[", document_id, "](/ui/apps/dynatrace.dashboards/dashboard/", document_id, ")")),
  else: if(category == "Workflows",
    if(startsWith(document_id, "(all"), "(all workflows — REST API)", else: concat("[", document_id, "](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/", document_id, ")")),
  else: if(category == "Notebooks",
    if(startsWith(document_id, "(all"), "(all notebooks — REST API)", else: concat("[", document_id, "](/ui/apps/dynatrace.notebooks/notebook/", document_id, ")")),
  else: if(category == "Anomaly Detectors",
    if(startsWith(document_id, "(all"), "(all anomaly detectors — REST API)", else: concat("[", document_id, "](/ui/intent/dynatrace.davis.anomalydetection/open_anomaly_detector_schema#%7B%22dt.settings.object_id%22%3A%22", document_id, "%22%2C%22dt.settings.schema_id%22%3A%22builtin%3Adavis.anomaly-detectors%22%7D)")),
  else: if(category == "API tokens",
    concat("[", document_id, "](/ui/apps/dynatrace.classic.tokens/ui/access-tokens?tokenID=", document_id, ")"),
  else: document_id)))))
| fields timeframe, interval, Type = category, Source, `Usage trend` = executions, `Total deprecated calls` = total, `Deprecated usages`
70.title: Classic REST & settings usage by caller

76.content:
### Alerting

---

Before upgrading, rework classic alerting configs — move **management zones** and **alerting rules** to **DQL-based alerting** and **workflow-based alert notifications**. Recreate metric-event alerts in [Anomaly Detection](/ui/apps/dynatrace.davis.anomalydetection/).

**Action**: [Migrate metric-event alerts to DQL-based anomaly detectors](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/platform/upgrade/metric-alerting).

77.description: Lower is better — should trend toward 0 as classic metric-event alerts move to DQL-based anomaly detectors.
77.title: Classic metric-event activity over time

78.content:
### Deprecated service & entity inventory (App Observability)

---

Service-side concepts that will not exist in Latest Dynatrace — calculated service metrics, external/opaque
services, database services, message-queue MEs. These rows show how much of each you have so you
can plan replacements.

79.title: Calculated service metrics — by service usage

80.content:
### Classic service detection & rule settings (App Observability)

---

SDv1 service-detection, failure-detection, request-attribute, and request-naming configuration
that uses configuration knobs not supported after upgrading (Management Zones, non-`primary_tags.` PG tags, service
tags). Each tile lists rules that must be reworked before upgrading.

81.description: Scopes listed here have Enhanced Endpoints for SDv1 disabled. Each entry will break when upgrading to Latest Dynatrace — enable Enhanced Endpoints for SDv1 on every scope shown before upgrading.
81.title: Enhanced Endpoints for SDv1 — scopes that will break when upgrading to Latest Dynatrace

82.title: Metric event alerts on classic metric selectors
82.description:
Metric-event alerts still using a classic metric selector. Every enabled one must be recreated as a DQL-based custom alert before upgrading — start at the top.

🔴 **Enabled · fired recently** — in active use; recreate first (sorted by recent alert volume). \
🟡 **Enabled** — quiet, or recent activity couldn't be loaded; still recreate it. \
⚪ **Disabled** — nothing to migrate.

83.title: Disk anomaly detection settings
83.description:
Classic disk anomaly detection is replaced by the new disk alerting when you upgrade. Each row is a scope — the environment default, then any host or host-group override that still needs action.

🔴 **Enabled** — this detection stops working after upgrade; recreate the equivalent in the new disk alerting first. \
🟢 **Disabled** — nothing to migrate. \
⚪ **Unknown** — the setting wasn't returned; open it to check.

86.description: External services (excluding databases). Latest Dynatrace has no separate external-service concept.

// External / opaque services (excluding databases) — Latest Dynatrace removes this concept.
fetch dt.entity.service
| filter isExternalService == true
| filter serviceType != "DATABASE_SERVICE"
| fields `Service` = entity.name, `Type` = serviceType, id
| limit 5000

| fields `Service` = entity.name, id

89.description: Classic SDv1 service-detection rules (full-web-request / full-web-service) that use management zones or non-`primary_tags.` PG_Tag conditions. Each row needs rework before upgrading to Latest Dynatrace.

// Detailed breakdown of Classic Dashboards usage over time per dashboard.
fetch dt.system.events

| filter dt.app.id == "dynatrace.classic.dashboards"
| filter isNotNull(details.page_dashboard_id)
| filterOut in(details.page_dashboard_id, "global", "default")
| makeTimeseries Usage = count(default:0),
    by:{details.page_dashboard_id},
    bins: 10
| fieldsAdd totalUsage = arraySum(Usage)

128.query:
// Detailed breakdown of Classic Dashboards usage per dashboard.
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.provider == "CLASSIC_APPS"
| filter dt.app.id == "dynatrace.classic.dashboards"
| filter event.type == "app.opened"
| filter isNotNull(details.page_dashboard_id)
| filterOut in(details.page_dashboard_id, "global", "default")
// Usage is the page-view count per bin (summed into Views below); UsersTrend is the
// distinct users per bin, so a user who opens a dashboard several times in a bin counts once.
| makeTimeseries Usage = count(default:0), UsersTrend = countDistinct(user.id, default:0),
    by:{details.page_dashboard_id},
    bins: 24
| fieldsAdd totalUsage = arraySum(Usage)
// Count the distinct users that opened each classic dashboard over the timeframe.
| lookup [
    fetch dt.system.events
    | filter event.provider == "CLASSIC_APPS"
    | filter dt.app.id == "dynatrace.classic.dashboards"
    | filter event.type == "app.opened"
    | filter isNotNull(details.page_dashboard_id)
    | filterOut in(details.page_dashboard_id, "global", "default")
    | sort timestamp desc
    | summarize uniqueUsers = countDistinct(user.id),
        latestName = takeFirst(details.page_dashboard_name),
        by:{details.page_dashboard_id}
  ], sourceField: details.page_dashboard_id, lookupField: details.page_dashboard_id
| fieldsAdd uniqueUsers = lookup.uniqueUsers, latestName = lookup.latestName
| sort uniqueUsers desc, totalUsage desc
| limit 100
| fieldsAdd `Classic dashboard` = concat(
    "[", coalesce(latestName, details.page_dashboard_id), "]",
    "(/ui/apps/dynatrace.classic.dashboards/#dashboard;id=", details.page_dashboard_id, ")")
| fields
    timeframe, interval,
    `Classic dashboard`,
    `Usage trend` = UsersTrend,
    `Views` = totalUsage,
    `Users` = uniqueUsers

130.content:
### Deprecated REST API & Settings

---

A number of APIs and Settings will be removed when upgrading to Latest Dynatrace. These are tied to features being retired - any REST call that reads or writes them will stop working after the upgrade and may require migration steps.

**Action**: Workflows that call deprecated Settings endpoints or REST API paths must be updated before upgrading. External systems authenticating with API keys (shown per token below) need to stop referencing the listed endpoints.

131.content:
### Classic entity model usage in DQL

---

DQL queries using deprecated entity selectors, grouped by source (Dashboards, Workflows, Notebooks, Anomaly Detectors, Segment variables, Site Reliability Guardians). Each row is a single document with a link to open it directly.

**Action:** Migrate DQL queries that reference the classic entity model (`fetch dt.entity.*`, `entityName()`, `entityAttr()`, `classicEntitySelector()`, or `dt.entity.*` dimensions). The migration skill available in the Dynatrace Assistant and via `dtctl` can guide the rewrite for each query.

132.query:
// Count of UNIQUE documents running classic entity-model DQL per day, series per consumer type
fetch dt.system.query_executions
| filter status == "SUCCEEDED"
| filter in(client.application_context, "dynatrace.dashboards", "dynatrace.automations", "dynatrace.notebooks", "dynatrace.site.reliability.guardian") or (client.application_context == "dynatrace.segments.management" and contains(client.client_context, "variables-data")) or contains(client.source, "anomaly_detection")
// Match executions the query engine flags as classic entity-model usage, or whose DQL text references classic entity constructs (entityName/entityAttr/classicEntitySelector/dt.entity.*).
| filter in("CLASSIC_ENTITY_MIGRATION_ADVISED", flags)
    or contains(query_string, "entityName(")
    or contains(query_string, "entityAttr(")
    or contains(query_string, "classicEntitySelector")
    or contains(query_string, "dt.entity.")
| fieldsAdd source = client.source
| parse source, "LD '/ui/dashboard/' [a-z0-9-]+:dashboard_id"
| parse source, "LD '/ui/notebook/' [a-z0-9-]+:notebook_id"
| parse source, "LD '/segments/segment/' [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+:segment_id"
| parse client.client_context, """LD '"dt.task.id":"' LD:task_id '"'"""
| fieldsAdd workflow_id = client.workflow_context
| fieldsAdd category = if(client.application_context == "dynatrace.dashboards", "Dashboards",
  else: if(client.application_context == "dynatrace.automations", "Workflows",
  else: if(client.application_context == "dynatrace.notebooks", "Notebooks",
  else: if(client.application_context == "dynatrace.segments.management", "Segment variables",
  else: if(client.application_context == "dynatrace.site.reliability.guardian", "Site Reliability Guardian",
  else: if(contains(client.source, "anomaly_detection"), "Anomaly Detectors",
  else: "Other"))))))
| fieldsAdd document_id = coalesce(dashboard_id, notebook_id, workflow_id, segment_id, task_id)
| filter isNotNull(document_id)
| fields timestamp, category, document_id
| makeTimeseries artifacts = countDistinct(document_id), default: 0, by: {category}, bins: 10

205.content:
### Log Classic

---

Log Classic is replaced by the Logs app powered by Grail, which adds powerful analytics and flexible retention — all Log Classic settings are migrated during the upgrade. Ingest into the classic store (Elasticsearch) means the upgrade has not yet completed. The tenant state is derived from observed log ingest by storage backend over the dashboard timeframe.

[Upgrade guide: Log Monitoring Classic to Logs powered by Grail](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/logs-upgrade-to-lma)

206.title: Log consumption: Classic vs Grail

207.description:
Each Kubernetes cluster's Dynatrace Operator readiness for the latest Dynatrace — one column per requirement (operator version, platform token, OneAgent mode, image pull, ActiveGate version). A cluster is ready only when every column is green.

🟢 **Met** — this requirement is satisfied. \
🔴 **Action required** — bring this requirement up to spec before upgrading.
207.query:
// Per-cluster readiness of the Dynatrace Operator, combining four checks:
// operator >= 1.10 (K8S_DEPLOYMENT), DynaKube platform token, OneAgent mode != classicFullStack,
// and image-pull / public-registry readiness (K8S_DYNAKUBE), joined by cluster.
smartscapeNodes K8S_DEPLOYMENT
| filter tags[`app.kubernetes.io/name`] == "dynatrace-operator" and tags[`app.kubernetes.io/component`] == "operator"
| fieldsAdd ver = tags[`app.kubernetes.io/version`]
| fieldsAdd ver = if(startsWith(ver, "v"), substring(ver, from: 1), else: ver)
| parse ver, "LONG:major '.' LONG:minor"
| fieldsAdd major = coalesce(major, 0), minor = coalesce(minor, 0)
| fieldsAdd op_ready = (major > 1 or (major == 1 and minor >= 10))
| summarize operator_version = takeFirst(tags[`app.kubernetes.io/version`]),
            operator_ready = (count() > 0 and countIf(op_ready == false) == 0),
    by: { cluster = k8s.cluster.name }
| join [
    smartscapeNodes K8S_DYNAKUBE
    | parse k8s.object, "JSON:config"
    | fieldsAdd dk_token = coalesce(config[`status`][`apiToken`][`platform`] == true, false)
    | fieldsAdd dk_mode_ready = isNull(config[`spec`][`oneAgent`][`classicFullStack`])
    | fieldsAdd dk_image_ready = coalesce((tags[`feature.dynatrace.com/node-image-pull`] == "true"
        or tags[`feature.dynatrace.com/use-public-registry`] == "true"
        or config[`status`][`apiToken`][`platform`] == true
        or isNotNull(config[`spec`][`oneAgent`][`cloudNativeFullStack`][`image`])
        or isNotNull(config[`spec`][`activeGate`][`image`])), false)
    | summarize token_ready = (countIf(dk_token == false) == 0),
                mode_ready = (countIf(dk_mode_ready == false) == 0),
                image_ready = (countIf(dk_image_ready == false) == 0),
        by: { cluster = k8s.cluster.name }
  ], on: { cluster }, fields: { token_ready, mode_ready, image_ready }
| fieldsAdd token_ready = coalesce(token_ready, false), mode_ready = coalesce(mode_ready, false), image_ready = coalesce(image_ready, false)
| join [
    fetch dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster
    | fields cluster = entity.name, activeGateVersion, cluster_id = id
  ], on: { cluster }, fields: { activeGateVersion, cluster_id }
| fieldsAdd ag_ready = (isNotNull(activeGateVersion) and activeGateVersion >= "1.341")
| fieldsAdd ready = operator_ready and token_ready and mode_ready and image_ready and ag_ready
| filter not ready
| fieldsAdd `Operator ≥1.10` = if(operator_ready, "🟢", else: "🔴"),
    `Platform token` = if(token_ready, "🟢", else: "🔴"),
    `OneAgent mode` = if(mode_ready, "🟢", else: "🔴"),
    `Image pull` = if(image_ready, "🟢", else: "🔴"),
    `ActiveGate ≥1.341` = if(ag_ready, "🟢", else: "🔴")
| fields `Cluster` = cluster, `Operator version` = operator_version, `Operator ≥1.10`, `Platform token`, `OneAgent mode`, `Image pull`, `ActiveGate ≥1.341`, id = cluster_id
| sort `Cluster` asc
207.title: Dynatrace Operator — clusters to migrate

208.query:
// Hosts running OneAgent below the minimum required version (< 1.343) — each must be upgraded before upgrade.
fetch dt.entity.host
| fields entity.name, installerVersion, osType, id
| filter isNotNull(installerVersion)
| fieldsAdd version_parts = splitString(installerVersion, ".")
| fieldsAdd major = version_parts[0], minor = toLong(version_parts[1])
| filter not (major == "1" and minor >= 343)
| fields `Host` = entity.name, `OneAgent version` = installerVersion, `OS` = osType, id
| sort `OneAgent version` asc
| limit 5000
208.title: OneAgent hosts to upgrade

221.query:
// Share of log ingest already on Grail (Logs app) over the dashboard timeframe.
// 100% means no classic-store (Elasticsearch) ingest remains; lower means Log Classic is still in use.
timeseries logs = sum(dt.sfm.server.log_and_events_monitoring.events_incoming_count),
  filter: { event.type == "LOG" },
  by: { storage_type },
  default: 0
| fieldsAdd total = arraySum(logs)
| fieldsAdd grail_total = if(storage_type == "Grail", total, else: 0)
| summarize grail = sum(grail_total), all = sum(total)
| fieldsAdd `Logs on Grail` = if(all > 0, concat(toString(toLong(toDouble(grail) / toDouble(all) * 100)), "%"), else: "No log ingest")
| fields `Logs on Grail`
221.title: Logs on Grail

222.description: Counts only **enabled** integrations — those actively in use, which must be recreated in the new model. Disabled integrations still migrate on upgrade and are shown separately in the chart. Lower is better — this count should trend toward **0** as you complete the migration.

227.description:

🔴 **Log Classic active** — still ingesting into Log Classic; move ingestion to Logs on Grail before upgrading. \

🟢 **Upgraded to Logs on Grail** — ready. \

230.content:
### Digital experience monitoring

Track your readiness to move Real User Monitoring and Synthetic monitoring to the latest Dynatrace, powered by Grail.

#### Real user monitoring (RUM)

The latest Dynatrace Real User Monitoring experience — powered by Grail — is the basis for frontend monitoring on the latest Dynatrace. Below is your full inventory of Web, Mobile, and Custom application frontends, and which ones already have the new RUM experience enabled.

**Need to enable RUM on more frontends?** Use the [Experience Vitals app](/ui/apps/dynatrace.experience.vitals) to manage frontend monitoring settings, see live coverage, and turn on the new RUM experience for the remaining apps.

**Using custom applications?** Custom application frontends aren't supported yet on the latest Dynatrace. Enable all eligible Web and Mobile frontends now, and hold off on migrating custom-app frontends — a migration path will be provided and this section updated once it's available.

300.content:
### Classic RBAC roles

---

Group permissions granted through classic role-based access control (RBAC) are being replaced by policies. Review which groups still rely on classic RBAC roles and transition them to default policies — the recommended best practice.

[Migrate roles to policies](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/manage/identity-access-management/permission-management/manage-user-permissions-policies/advanced/migrate-roles)

301.description: Lower is better — this count should trend toward **0** as you complete the migration.
301.query:
// Groups still assigned permissions through classic RBAC roles — latest count with daily trend, from the IAM readiness snapshot.
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "PLATFORM_PERMISSION_READINESS_EVENT"
| makeTimeseries trend = max(arraySize(legacy_permissions_groups)), time: timestamp, bins: 24
| fieldsAdd `Groups on classic RBAC roles` = arrayLast(arrayRemoveNulls(trend))
301.title: Groups on classic RBAC roles

302.query:
// Groups already using default policies — latest count with daily trend, from the IAM readiness snapshot.
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "PLATFORM_PERMISSION_READINESS_EVENT"
| makeTimeseries trend = max(arraySize(default_policies_groups)), time: timestamp, bins: 24
| fieldsAdd `Groups using default policies` = arrayLast(arrayRemoveNulls(trend))
302.title: Groups using default policies

303.query:
// Groups on classic RBAC roles over time, from the daily IAM readiness snapshots.
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "PLATFORM_PERMISSION_READINESS_EVENT"
| makeTimeseries `Classic RBAC roles` = max(arraySize(legacy_permissions_groups)), time: timestamp, bins: 10

304.query:
// Groups still assigned permissions through classic RBAC roles, from the latest IAM readiness snapshot.
// Each group links to its account-management page; the account is taken from the readiness event.
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "PLATFORM_PERMISSION_READINESS_EVENT"
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 1
| fields account_uuid = account.uuid, grp = legacy_permissions_groups
| expand grp
| parse grp, "JSON:obj"
| fieldsAdd Group = obj[name],
    `Action` = concat("[Open my account](https://myaccount.dynatrace.com/account/iam/group-management?account-uuid=", account_uuid, "&groupUuid=", obj[uuid], ")")
| fields Group, `Action`
| sort Group asc

305.content:
### ActiveGate & network readiness

---

ActiveGates and network configuration must be in the right state before upgrading to Latest Dynatrace. Multi-environment ActiveGates are no longer supported, every ActiveGate needs version 1.225+ with a valid auth token, and network zones are switched always-on.

**Action**: Replace multi-environment ActiveGates with per-tenant Environment ActiveGates, [upgrade ActiveGates to 1.225+ and set a valid auth token](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/ingest-from/dynatrace-activegate/activegate-security), and [review your network zones](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/manage/network-zones/network-zones-basic-info) so OneAgent routing is unaffected when they are enabled.

306.title: Multi-environment ActiveGates
306.description: Lower is better — this count should trend toward **0** as you complete the migration.

309.title: Network zones readiness
309.description:
Overall network-zone readiness for this environment. See the checks table for the specific items to act on.

🟢 **Ready** — network zones enabled. \
🔴 **Action required** — enable network zones so defined zones and pinned ActiveGates keep routing after upgrade.

310.title: ActiveGates to upgrade
310.description:
ActiveGates and the state of the authentication token they need to upgrade.

🔴 **Absent / Invalid / Unsupported** — fix or reissue the ActiveGate's auth token before upgrading. \
🟡 **Expiring** — renew the auth token soon. \
🟢 **Valid** — ready. \
⚪ **Unknown** — token state couldn't be determined.

311.title: Network zones readiness checks
311.description:
Whether network-zone routing is ready for upgrade.

🔴 **Enable before upgrade / Review** — defined zones or pinned ActiveGates would misroute after upgrade unless network zones are enabled; enable them and re-check. \
🟢 **Ready** — network zones enabled. \
⚪ **Not required / n/a** — nothing to act on for this row.

312.title: ActiveGate readiness
312.description:
Share of ActiveGates by upgrade readiness.

🟢 **Ready** — version 1.225+ with a valid auth token. \
🔴 **Below 1.225** — upgrade the ActiveGate to the minimum version first. \
🟡 **Token needs attention** — reissue or renew the auth token.

313.description: Lower is better — this count should trend toward **0** as you complete the migration.
313.title: Guardians to migrate

314.description:
Share of Site Reliability Guardians by classic-SLO status.

🟢 **Migrated** — no classic SLO references remain. \
🔴 **Needs migration** — an objective still references a classic SLO; repoint it before upgrading.
314.title: Guardians by SLO status

315.description:
Share of classic issue-tracking integrations by state.

🔴 **Enabled** — actively in use; recreate in the new model first. \
⚪ **Disabled** — inactive; nothing to recreate.
315.title: Integrations by state

316.description:
Share of Kubernetes clusters by monitoring readiness.

🟢 **Ready** — every monitoring feature is on. \
🔴 **Action required** — a monitoring feature is disabled; turn it on before upgrading.
316.title: Cluster monitoring readiness

317.description:
Share of scopes — the environment default plus any host or host-group override — by classic disk anomaly detection state.

🟢 **Disabled** — nothing to migrate. \
🔴 **Enabled** — classic disk detection still on; recreate it in the new disk alerting before upgrading.
317.title: Disk detection status

318.description:
Share of logs and business-event records by ingest pipeline — a snapshot of the classic-vs-OpenPipeline split.

🟢 **OpenPipeline** — processed by an OpenPipeline route; migrated. \
🔴 **Classic Pipeline** — still on the legacy default route; migrate this volume before classic processing is switched off.
318.title: Records by ingest pipeline
